reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully encodes the informal claim. `[Field K] [Infinite K]` captures "infinite field F"; `[AddCommGroup V] [Module K V]` is the standard encoding of a vector space over K. The finite family of proper subspaces is encoded as `γ : ι → Submodule K V` with `[Fintype ι]` and the hypothesis `∀ i, γ i ≠ ⊤` (a submodule is proper iff it is not the whole space, i.e., ≠ ⊤). The conclusion `(⋃ i, (γ i : Set V)) ≠ ⊤` asserts the union is not all of V, since `(⊤ : Set V) = Set.univ` and `((⊤ : Submodule K V) : Set V) = Set.univ`. The universal quantification over K, V, ι, γ correctly matches "for any finite collection of proper subspaces of any vector space over any infinite field". No hypothesis is silently added or dropped.
